title: nerve-wml v1.2.0: Substrate-Agnostic Nerve Protocol for Inter-WML Communication
description: v1.1.0 release extends the substrate-agnostic nerve protocol to THREE structurally distinct substrates (MlpWML dense MLP, LifWML surrogate-gradient spiking, TransformerWML attention-based) and adds direct empirical measurement of inter-substrate information transmission. Key findings (v1.1.0, April 2026): four-point scaling law for the polymorphism gap (median 10.7% at N=2 shrinking to ~2.4% at N=32 and plateau at N=64, 15/15 direction-stable seeds); mutual information MI/H between substrate-emitted codes = 0.91 at N=1 strengthening to 0.96 at N=16 pool scale (192 cross-pair measurements); round-trip fidelity MLP-LIF-MLP = 0.99; cross-substrate merge ratio 0.97 (frozen LIF recovers MLP task competence from emitted codes alone). These upgrade Claim B (substrate-agnostic information transmission) from architectural postulate to empirical finding. Eleven gates from v1.0.0 continue to validate: protocol correctness (Gate P), MLP-LIF polymorphism (Gate W), merge fine-tuning (Gate M), resolved scientific shortcuts (Gate M2), scaling (Gate Scale), neuroletter-semantics (Gate Interp), INT8 Loihi/Akida export (Gate Neuro), dream-of-kiki consolidation (Gate Dream partial), adaptive alphabet (Gate Adaptive), and micro-kiki LLM advisor (Gate LLM Advisor). Paper drafts v0.3 through v0.8 track the iterations. Cited in the parent research program's Paper 1 section 7.4 (dreamOfkiki cross-substrate validation).
